question.text: When did the successfully redesigned cash-settled single-bond IRF contracts launch in India?

answer.text: December 2013 (revised guidelines leading to successful launch of 10-year GOI bond futures)

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The December 2013 revised guidelines introduced cash-settled futures on a single GOI bond (10-year), which proved to be the successful format. Earlier attempts in 2003, 2009, and 2011 had limited success or failed.
**The Trap:** June 2003 was the first (failed) attempt. August 2009 was the second attempt with CTD mechanism (also failed). March 2011 saw launch of 91-day T-Bill and 2Y/5Y bond futures with limited success. December 2013 was the breakthrough.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The timeline of IRF launches is specifically tested in NISM Series IV: 2003 (fail) → 2009 (fail) → 2011 (limited) → 2013 (success) → 2015 (extension to 6Y, 13Y) → 2023 (corporate bond index futures).
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 2013 success was due to three key changes: (1) cash settlement instead of physical delivery, (2) single specific bond instead of notional bond with CTD, (3) broader participation framework. These addressed all known issues from prior attempts.
